prompt:
The following are the beginning sentences of a news article from the Guardian.
-------
 Labour has discarded its social care commitments and seems set to repeat the failure of previous governments to tackle the care crisis, the sector’s leaders have warned. Announcements by Rachel Reeves, the chancellor, that she would not cap costs for older people’s care and Stephen Kinnock, the care minister, that he would scrap a fund giving training for 37,000 care workers, have alarmed the social care sector. Writing in the Observer, the chief executives of Care England (CE), the Homecare Association (HA) and the National Care Forum (NCF) say ministers have chosen to prioritise the NHS over social care, while failing to recognise they are interdependent. Labour’s manifesto included pledges to introduce a National Care Service and other elements of “deep reform” in its manifesto. Reeves will deliver her first budget on 30 October and has blamed the Conservatives for leaving a £22bn black hole in the public finances, but the leaders say that social care faces a set of problems that require urgent attention. “For decades, successive UK governments have failed to take responsibility for a growing social care crisis. This government must not continue that pattern,” write Prof Martin Green of CE, Vic Rayner of NCF, Dr Jane Towson of HA and Suhail Mirza, non-executive director of Newcross Healthcare. “Yet, in a little over one month in office, the government has done [and] said little to suggest that social care under their watch will fare much better, a view reinforced by the deafening silence on social care in Sir Keir Starmer’s speech on 27 August. We urge the government to change course.” Care homes and homecare providers have been operating with staff vacancies of more than 100,000 for several years, making it increasingly hard for families of older people and those with disabilities to find help. Carers can often make more money in supermarkets and staff turnover is high.
-------
Please paraphrase based on the sentences provided.  Wrap your entire response with double quotation marks. Do not include keywords ['attempt', 'bench', 'eye', 'food', 'value'] in the response. Answer with at least 137 words. Your response should contain less than 20 sentences. Your response must have 1 sections. Mark the beginning of each section with SECTION X, such as:
SECTION 1
[content of section 1]
SECTION 2
[content of section 2]
response:
"SECTION 1

Labour's plans for social care have been abandoned, and the sector's leaders are warning that the government may repeat the mistakes of previous administrations. The sector's leaders are alarmed by recent announcements from the chancellor and care minister, which they believe will exacerbate the care crisis. The care sector is facing significant challenges, including staff vacancies of over 100,000 and high staff turnover rates.

The sector's leaders are urging the government to change course and address the care crisis, which they believe requires urgent attention. They point out that the government has failed to recognize the interdependence of the NHS and social care, and that the care sector has been neglected for decades. The leaders are calling on the government to take responsibility for the growing social care crisis and to introduce meaningful reforms.

The care sector is facing significant challenges, including staff vacancies of over 100,000 and high staff turnover rates. Carers can often earn more money in supermarkets than in the care sector, making it difficult to attract and retain staff. The sector's leaders are urging the government to take action to address these challenges and to introduce meaningful reforms to the care system.

The government's failure to address the care crisis is a major concern, and the sector's leaders are warning that the consequences will be severe if action is not taken. The care sector is a vital part of the UK's healthcare system, and its neglect has serious consequences for older people and those with disabilities. The sector's leaders are urging the government to take responsibility for the care crisis and to introduce meaningful reforms to the care system."

prompt:
The following are the beginning sentences of a news article from the Guardian.
-------
 The Reform UK MP James McMurdock was apparently jailed as a teenager for repeatedly kicking his then girlfriend, it has emerged, despite him previously claiming she was hurt after he pushed her. According to court records released to the Times, McMurdock spent 21 days in a young offender institution after kicking the victim “around four times” in an incident outside a nightclub in Chelmsford when he was 19. McMurdock, who became Reform’s fifth MP after winning the Essex seat of South Basildon and East Thurrock by 98 votes at the general election, gave a different account of events shortly after his victory. Later in July, the mother of his former girlfriend told the Mail that the new MP was “a monster” who had “left marks on her body”, saying: “It took two security guards to pull him off her.” In response to her comments, McMurdock told the paper: “A generous person might call it a teenage indiscretion, but I do not expect everyone to be so kind. Nearly 20 years ago, at 19 years of age, at the end of a night out together, we argued and I pushed her. “She fell over and she was hurt. Despite being 38 now and having lived a whole life again, I still feel deeply ashamed of that moment and apologetic. Despite us both being very drunk, I handed myself into the police immediately and admitted my fault.” According to a copy of the court register extract released to the Times, the magistrate explained that McMurdock was given a custodial sentence that “was not suspended in light of serious nature of the offence”. Such an offence “requires immediate punishment”, the extract said, adding that a pre-sentence report appeared to show a “lack of willingness to comply”. McMurdock initially denied the charge of assault but changed his plea just before the trial was about to start, the Mail said.
-------
Please explain in simpler terms what this text means.  Your answer must contain a title, wrapped in double angular brackets, such as <<poem of joy>>. Do not include keywords ['awareness', 'door', 'plate', 'staff', 'village'] in the response. Your response should contain less than 15 sentences.
response:
<<A Troubling Past for a New MP>>

A British politician named James McMurdock has been accused of a serious crime from his past. When he was 19 years old, he kicked his girlfriend repeatedly outside a nightclub. This led to him being sent to a young offender institution for 21 days. 

McMurdock initially denied the charge, but later admitted to it just before his trial. He claimed that his girlfriend fell over and was hurt after he pushed her, not after he kicked her. However, a court record shows that he was given a custodial sentence because the offense was serious.

McMurdock's girlfriend's mother described him as a "monster" who left marks on her body. McMurdock apologized for his actions and said he feels ashamed. He became a member of parliament after winning an election by just 98 votes.

prompt:
The following are the beginning sentences of a news article from the Guardian.
-------
 Oasis have issued a warning to people against reselling tickets for their reunion tour – or buying those resold tickets – on the secondary market at vastly inflated prices. The band said tickets “sold in breach of the terms and conditions will be cancelled by the promoters”. A limited number of people were able to secure the coveted tickets through a presale ballot on Friday evening. But, shortly after going on sale, floor standing tickets were available from about £807 to £3,615 on the StubHub website, while seats in some lower level sections were on offer at £6,347. Viagogo, another resale website, also advertised tickets for more than £2,000. Officially issued tickets range in price from £73 to £151, with the most expensive official package for the Wembley concerts retailing at £506.25. Oasis wrote on X: “We have noticed people attempting to sell tickets on the secondary market since the start of the pre-sale. “Please note, tickets can ONLY be resold, at face value, via @Ticketmaster and @Twickets. Tickets sold in breach of the terms and conditions will be cancelled by the promoters.” Lisa Webb, a consumer law expert at Which?, said: “Oasis fans are understandably eager to snap up tickets to the reunion tour, but we’d strongly advise against buying any of the resale tickets currently popping up online at inflated prices. “Not only is there a chance that some of these listings could be scam attempts, but even legitimate tickets could be cancelled, rendering them invalid, if they are sold outside of the official resale platforms or at above face value.” Tickets for the band’s 15 UK shows in London, Manchester, Edinburgh and Cardiff will go on sale at 9am on Saturday, while the sale for their two Dublin gigs starts at 8am.
-------
Please generate a story based on the sentences provided.  Your response should contain at least 14 sentences.
response:
Oasis, the iconic British rock band, has issued a stern warning to fans against reselling tickets for their highly anticipated reunion tour on the secondary market at inflated prices. The band's management team has made it clear that tickets "sold in breach of the terms and conditions will be cancelled by the promoters," leaving fans who purchase these tickets at risk of being left empty-handed.

The warning comes as fans eagerly await the official ticket sale for the band's 15 UK shows in London, Manchester, Edinburgh, and Cardiff, as well as their two Dublin gigs. The tickets, which range in price from £73 to £151, are expected to sell out quickly, with the most expensive official package for the Wembley concerts retailing at £506.25.

However, fans who are desperate to secure tickets have been taking to the secondary market, with some reselling floor-standing tickets for as much as £3,615 and seats in lower level sections for over £6,347. Viagogo, another resale website, is also advertising tickets for more than £2,000, leaving many fans wondering if these prices are worth the risk.

Lisa Webb, a consumer law expert at Which?, has advised fans against buying resale tickets at inflated prices, warning that there is a chance that some of these listings could be scam attempts. Even legitimate tickets could be cancelled, rendering them invalid, if they are sold outside of the official resale platforms or at above face value.

Oasis fans are understandably eager to snap up tickets to the reunion tour, but the band's management team is urging them to be cautious and only purchase tickets through the official channels. The band has made it clear that tickets can only be resold at face value via Ticketmaster and Twickets, and that any tickets sold in breach of the terms and conditions will be cancelled by the promoters.

The official ticket sale for the Oasis reunion tour is set to take place on Saturday, with tickets going on sale at 9am for the UK shows and 8am for the Dublin gigs. Fans are advised to act quickly, as tickets are expected to sell out rapidly. In the meantime, fans are being warned to be cautious of resale websites and to only purchase tickets through the official channels to avoid any potential risks.

As the anticipation builds for the Oasis reunion tour, fans are left wondering if the risks of buying resale tickets are worth the reward. With the band's management team taking a firm stance against ticket touting, fans are being urged to be patient and wait for the official ticket sale to take place.

prompt:
The following are the beginning sentences of a news article from the Guardian.
-------
 There feels inevitability around Celtic winning – or at least not losing – the first Old Firm meeting of the season. What will follow after dust settles on a crash, bang, wallop occasion at Celtic Park is more surprising – outbreaks of sense and sensibility on either half of Glasgow’s football divide. Brendan Rodgers appears to have realised the world outside Scotland shrugs shoulders when Celtic prevail on the domestic scene. The manager has placed Champions League competitiveness, or progress, at the forefront of his aspirations for this campaign. He has directly stated he wants his team to aim for a playoff place from the new format. This attitude is long overdue: for more than a decade, Celtic have made up the numbers in Europe. “We don’t want to just participate,” says Rodgers. The manager is “excited” by the challenge rather than in awe of it. Looking beyond parochialism is not only essential to keep Rodgers stimulated but sensible in respect of an uneven financial playing field. Celtic warmed up for the visit of Rangers by tossing aside St Mirren.
-------
Please summarize based on the sentences provided.  There should be 1 paragraphs. Paragraphs are separated with the markdown divider: ***
response:
*** 

Celtic's upcoming match against Rangers is expected to be a significant event, with Celtic likely to win or at least not lose. However, the real surprise may come after the match, as Celtic manager Brendan Rodgers has set his sights on more than just domestic success. He aims to have Celtic compete in the Champions League playoffs, a goal that has been long overdue, as the team has been making up the numbers in Europe for over a decade. Rodgers' attitude shift is seen as a positive step, as it acknowledges the uneven financial playing field in Scottish football and looks beyond parochialism.
